text.title: Message Queue Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real mq-patterns deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Service uses pub/sub when it should be work-queue (or vice versa); messages processed by the wrong consumer set.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skipped the topology decision and copied the first example found.
antipattern.fix: Write the topology contract first; one message → one consumer is work-queue; one message → every subscriber is fan-out.

antipattern.symptom: Poison message blocks the queue (infinite retry) or is dropped on max-retries with no audit trail.
antipattern.fix: Every queue that can receive failure has a DLQ + alert before going live.

antipattern.symptom: At-least-once delivery + non-idempotent consumer = double-charge / duplicate emails in production.
antipattern.root-cause: Author assumed exactly-once delivery from the broker.
antipattern.fix: Always design consumers for idempotency; pick payload-key / natural-key / broker-key + dedup store.
